2603.28718.title: Stepwise Credit Assignment for GRPO on Flow-Matching Models
2603.28718.paper_summary: Flow-GRPO successfully applies reinforcement learning to flow models, but uses uniform credit assignment across all steps. This ignores the temporal structure of diffusion generation: early steps determine composition and content (low-frequency structure), while late steps resolve details and textures (high-frequency details). Moreover, assigning uniform credit based solely on the final image can inadvertently reward suboptimal intermediate steps, especially when errors are corrected later in the diffusion trajectory. We propose Stepwise-Flow-GRPO, which assigns credit based on each step's reward improvement. By leveraging Tweedie's formula to obtain intermediate reward estimates and introducing gain-based advantages, our method achieves superior sample efficiency and faster convergence. We also introduce a DDIM-inspired SDE that improves reward quality while preserving stochasticity for policy gradients.
2603.28718.paper_summary_zh: Flow-GRPO 成功地將強化學習應用於流模型，但在所有步驟中使用均勻的信用分配。這忽略了擴散生成的時間結構：早期步驟決定組成和內容（低頻結構），而晚期步驟解決細節和紋理（高頻細節）。此外，僅根據最終圖像分配均勻的信用可能會無意中獎勵次優的中間步驟，特別是在擴散軌跡中後期修正錯誤的情況下。我們提出了 Stepwise-Flow-GRPO，根據每一步的獎勵改善來分配信用。通過利用 Tweedie 的公式來獲取中間獎勵估計並引入基於增益的優勢，我們的方法實現了更優的樣本效率和更快的收斂。我們還引入了一種受 DDIM 啟發的 SDE，該方法在保留隨機性的同時提高了獎勵質量，以便用於策略梯度。
2603.28718.author: Yash Savani et.al.
2603.28718.authors: Yash Savani, Branislav Kveton, Yuchen Liu, Yilin Wang, Jing Shi, Subhojyoti Mukherjee, Nikos Vlassis, Krishna Kumar Singh
